summary: Russian forces conducted extensive drone strikes across multiple Ukrainian regions including Kharkiv, Sumy, Pavlohrad, and Mykolaiv, while Ukrainian drones targeted energy infrastructure in Zaporizhzhia and Kherson regions. Ground combat continued on the Sumy axis with Russian forces advancing in the Shostka and Sumy districts, and the Russian Defense Ministry claimed liberation of Bohodarivka in Dnipropetrovsk Oblast. A Russian Tu-160 strategic bomber was redeployed from the Far East to Engels airbase for equipping with cruise missiles.
key_points: Russian forces liberated Bohodarivka in Dnipropetrovsk Oblast, according to the Russian Defense Ministry. (Source: @tass_agency/382891)
Russian Defense Ministry reported VKS RF destroyed up to 95 Ukrainian servicemembers in Kostiantynivka and over 40 in Krasnyi Liman in 24 hours. (Source: @tass_agency/382892)

Russian tactical aviation launched guided aviation bombs on Sumy Oblast, while multiple UAV groups targeted Dnipropetrovsk, Kharkiv, Chernihiv, and Poltava regions. (Source: @kpszsu/66720, @kpszsu/66750, @eRadarrua/98573)

Ukrainian drones damaged energy facilities in Zaporizhzhia region causing widespread outages, and all districts of Kherson region are completely or partially without power. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31237)
A Russian Tu-160 strategic bomber was redeployed from the Far East to Engels airbase for equipping with cruise missiles. (Source: @eRadarrua/98588)
Russian FPV drone attacked a minibus in Zaporizhzhia, killing two people and wounding six including a child. (Source: @UkraineNow/82257)
SBU detained two Russian agents suspected of preparing a terrorist attack targeting a Ukrainian military member near Maidan Nezalezhnosti in central Kyiv. (Source: @UkraineNow/82258)
In Irkutsk, the governor officially recognized fuel has run out with shortages reaching 80%, most gas stations closed, and Rosneft stations have kilometer-long queues. (Source: @eRadarrua/98606)

sections.executive_summary: Russian forces claimed liberation of Bohodarivka in Dnipropetrovsk Oblast and reported inflicting over 1,500 casualties on Ukrainian forces across all sectors in 24 hours. Extensive drone warfare continued with Russian UAVs targeting Sumy, Dnipropetrovsk, Kharkiv, Chernihiv, and Poltava regions, while Ukrainian drones struck energy infrastructure in Zaporizhzhia and Kherson regions causing widespread blackouts. A Russian Tu-160 strategic bomber was redeployed from the Far East to Engels airbase for equipping with cruise missiles, signaling potential escalation in long-range strike posture.

points: Ukrainian drones struck energy facilities in Zaporizhzhia region, causing emergency power outages across a significant part of the region, while all districts of neighboring Kherson region are completely or partially without power. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31237)

SBU detained two Russian female agents suspected of preparing a terrorist attack in central Kyiv, planning to blow up a Ukrainian military member in his car near Maidan Nezalezhnosti pedestrian zone. One suspect is a former bakery owner in Sevastopol recruited after business debts. (Source: @UkraineNow/82258)
Russian FPV drone attacked a minibus in Zaporizhzhia, killing two people and wounding six including a child. (Source: @UkraineNow/82257)
Russian forces attacked a private enterprise in Dnipro, injuring seven people including a 22-year-old man in serious condition. (Source: @UkraineNow/82256)

Ukraine will require nearly $146 billion in external financing in 2026-2029, Finance Minister Sergey Marchenko announced following meetings with UK and Lithuanian finance ministers at a forum in Gdansk. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31224)

Heatwaves are sharply increasing electricity consumption in Ukraine while some equipment remains under repair after Russian attacks, according to Yasno CEO Serhiy Kovalenko. (Source: @UkraineNow/82250)
Local residents are resisting military commissars, with videos filmed in Kryvyi Rih and Zaporizhzhia. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31232)

Russian Defense Ministry reported VKS RF struck Ukrainian fuel-energy and transport infrastructure objects, and destroyed Ukrainian long-range UAV storage and launch sites over 24 hours. (Source: @tass_agency/382892)
Russian air defense shot down 12 guided aviation bombs and 415 Ukrainian fixed-wing UAVs over 24 hours, according to the Defense Ministry. (Source: @tass_agency/382892)
A Tu-160 strategic bomber was redeployed from the Far East to Engels airbase for equipping with cruise missiles. (Source: @eRadarrua/98588)

In Irkutsk, the governor officially recognized fuel has run out with shortages reaching 80%, most gas stations closed, and Rosneft stations have kilometer-long queues with people sleeping at stations. (Source: @eRadarrua/98606)
Fuel limits of 30 liters of gasoline and 200 liters of diesel per vehicle were introduced at state gas stations in multiple districts of Yakutia including Yakutsk. (Source: @tass_agency/382880)
A kamikaze drone struck a regular bus in the village of Petryatinka, Zlynkovsky district, Bryansk region, wounding a woman. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31237)

Russian Foreign Intelligence Service (SVR) claimed Kyiv is increasing cooperation with major Mexican drug cartels and seeking income from drug transit. (Source: @tass_agency/382886, @tass_agency/382883)
Russian forces liberated Bohodarivka in Dnipropetrovsk Oblast, according to the Russian Defense Ministry. (Source: @tass_agency/382891)

Russian Defense Ministry reported VKS RF destroyed up to 95 Ukrainian servicemembers in Kostiantynivka and over 40 in Krasnyi Liman in 24 hours, and in Krasnyi Liman advancing westward occupied 48 buildings. (Source: @tass_agency/382892)

Multiple UAV groups were tracked along the Dnipropetrovsk-Kharkiv border heading toward Orilka, with additional drones over Pavlohrad, Synelnykove, Berestyn, and a reactive UAV near Lozova in Kharkiv region. (Source: @eRadarrua/98572, @eRadarrua/98573, @eRadarrua/98596)
Russian forces launched KAB guided aviation bombs on Malokaterynivka in Zaporizhzhia region. (Source: @eRadarrua/98596)
Russian FPV drone attacked a minibus in Zaporizhzhia, killing two people and wounding six including a child. (Source: @UkraineNow/82257)
Drones were tracked toward Nikopol in Dnipropetrovsk Oblast from the east, and a probable reconnaissance UAV was heading toward Samarska Bay from Pavlohrad. (Source: @kpszsu/66766, @eRadarrua/98605)

On the Sumy axis, Russian assault troops in Shostka district continue small-arms battles in Bachevsk and surroundings; in Sumy district fighting in Pysarevka, Marino, and Novaya Sech; in Krasnopillia district fighting continues in forest areas. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31237)
Russian tactical aviation launched guided aviation bombs on Sumy Oblast. (Source: @kpszsu/66720)

Multiple UAVs tracked in Sumy region including three in the Trostianets-Chupakhivka area (two reactive), drones toward Yampil from northeast, past Karlivka heading northwest, and reactive drones through Haimaron into Sumy region and from Sumy region to Zinkiv. (Source: @eRadarrua/98602, @kpszsu/66772, @kpszsu/66723, @eRadarrua/98596)
Drones tracked along the Sumy-Chernihiv border heading south, and toward Bohodukhiv in Kharkiv region from northeast. (Source: @kpszsu/66769)
In Chernihiv region, Lightning-type UAVs were tracked in the Parafiivka/Ichnia area, and drones from Dmitrivka toward Pryluky district. (Source: @eRadarrua/98569, @eRadarrua/98568)
In Belgorod region's Rakitnyansky district, settlement of Proletarsky, an FPV drone incident was reported. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31237)

Ukrainian drones damaged energy facilities in Zaporizhzhia region causing widespread outages, and all districts of Kherson region are completely or partially without power. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31237)

Crimea and Sevastopol repelled the drone attack but air raid warnings were again sounding in Sevastopol by morning with at least 2 UAVs shot down, and the Crimean Bridge has been closed since night. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31237)
Two controlled UAVs were tracked from sea waters toward Ochakiv. (Source: @eRadarrua/98623)
A hostile UAV was tracked toward Kherson city from the south. (Source: @kpszsu/66722)
Russian forces launched KAB guided aviation bombs on Malokaterynivka in Zaporizhzhia region. (Source: @eRadarrua/98596)
Drones were tracked toward Zaporizhzhia city and toward Nikopol in Dnipropetrovsk Oblast from the east. (Source: @kpszsu/66750, @kpszsu/66766)
Russian tactical aviation launched guided aviation bombs (KABs) on Sumy Oblast and on Malokaterynivka in Zaporizhzhia region. (Source: @kpszsu/66720, @eRadarrua/98596)
Russian air defense shot down 12 Ukrainian guided aviation bombs and 415 Ukrainian fixed-wing UAVs over 24 hours, according to the Russian Defense Ministry. (Source: @tass_agency/382892)
A Tu-160 strategic bomber was redeployed from the Far East to Engels airbase for equipping with cruise missiles. (Source: @eRadarrua/98588)
Russian Defense Ministry reported strikes on Ukrainian long-range UAV storage and launch sites over 24 hours. (Source: @tass_agency/382892)
Ballistic weapon use threat was announced from the southeast and later called off. (Source: @kpszsu/66754, @kpszsu/66760)

Multiple UAV groups tracked across Sumy, Dnipropetrovsk, Kharkiv, Chernihiv, and Poltava regions including reactive-type drones and Lightning-type UAVs. (Source: @eRadarrua/98602, @eRadarrua/98569, @eRadarrua/98573, @kpszsu/66769)

key_developments: Russian forces claimed liberation of Bohodarivka in Dnipropetrovsk Oblast and reported VKS RF inflicted up to 95 casualties in Kostiantynivka and over 40 in Krasnyi Liman in 24 hours, with Ukrainian total losses claimed at over 1,500 across all sectors. (Source: @tass_agency/382891, @tass_agency/382892)

Ukrainian drone strikes damaged energy facilities in Zaporizhzhia region causing widespread outages, with all Kherson region districts completely or partially without power, and Crimean Bridge closed since night with air raid warnings in Sevastopol. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31237)

Russian tactical aviation launched KAB guided bombs on Sumy Oblast and Malokaterynivka in Zaporizhzhia region, while extensive UAV swarms targeted Dnipropetrovsk, Kharkiv, Chernihiv, and Poltava regions. (Source: @kpszsu/66720, @eRadarrua/98596, @eRadarrua/98573)

A Tu-160 strategic bomber was redeployed from the Far East to Engels airbase for equipping with cruise missiles, indicating potential preparation for long-range strike operations. (Source: @eRadarrua/98588)

SBU detained two Russian female agents preparing a terrorist attack in central Kyiv targeting a Ukrainian military member near Maidan Nezalezhnosti; one suspect was a former Sevastopol bakery owner recruited through debt leverage. (Source: @UkraineNow/82258)

Russian FPV drone attacked a minibus in Zaporizhzhia killing two and wounding six including a child; Russian forces also attacked a private enterprise in Dnipro injuring seven including a 22-year-old in serious condition. (Source: @UkraineNow/82257, @UkraineNow/82256)

In Irkutsk, the governor officially recognized fuel shortages reaching 80% with most gas stations closed and Rosneft stations having kilometer-long queues; Yakutia introduced fuel limits of 30L gasoline and 200L diesel per vehicle. (Source: @eRadarrua/98606, @tass_agency/382880)
sections.threat_assessment: The operational tempo remains high across multiple axes with Russia conducting combined drone and aviation strikes while claiming territorial gains in Dnipropetrovsk Oblast. The redeployment of a Tu-160 to Engels for cruise missile equipping suggests potential escalation in long-range strike operations. Ukrainian drone capabilities continue to threaten Russian-held territories including Crimea, with energy infrastructure remaining a primary target causing significant disruption. The SBU's foiled terrorist plot in central Kyiv indicates Russian intelligence services are actively pursuing asymmetric operations behind Ukrainian lines. Internal Russian fuel shortages in Irkutsk and Yakutia may constrain logistics if they spread.
sections.regional_response: Ukraine's financing needs remain critical with $146 billion required for 2026-2029 as announced by Finance Minister Marchenko at the Gdansk forum with UK and Lithuanian counterparts. Peru has opened a human trafficking investigation after dozens of its citizens were lured to Russia with false promises of rear-area jobs paying $20,000 bonuses and $4,000 monthly salaries, only to be sent to the front in Ukraine as cannon fodder. Putin and Lukashenko discussed Ukraine informally with no messages relayed from Zelenskyy, according to Peskov. The US and Iran agreed to halt mutual strikes and reopen the Strait of Hormuz, with further talks likely in Qatar.
sections.intelligence_notes: The Russian Defense Ministry's claim of over 1,500 Ukrainian casualties in 24 hours across all sectors should be treated as unverified propaganda until corroborated by independent sources. The redeployment of strategic aviation assets (Tu-160) to Engels warrants monitoring for potential cruise missile strikes. Russian SVR allegations of Ukrainian cooperation with Mexican drug cartels are likely disinformation operations aimed at undermining Western support. The fuel crisis in Irkutsk reaching 80% shortages with kilometer-long queues at Rosneft stations indicates sanctions impact on Russian domestic logistics. Peruvian citizen recruitment networks represent a new foreign fighter pipeline that may expand if not disrupted by law enforcement.
